title: The Poems of William of Shoreham (seven-poem edited selection)
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan item BAU0088.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR and Level 1 TEI encoding of a seven-poem selection from Matthias Konrath's 1902 EETS edition. The XML documents an immediate historical production layer of 274 consecutively named 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images; current survival and retrieval of that image set remain unverified, and an Internet Archive package endpoint is dark. Konrath re-edited the target poems directly from British Library Add MS 17376 with substantial editorial intervention and manuscript proof collation; his seven-poem text occupies pp.1-160, while the encoding omits the continuous notes on pp.161-246. Hanna identifies nine Latin leaves formerly at the front of Add MS 17376 part I as now separately preserved as Bodleian MS Lat. th. e. 32; that fragment contains no target poem. Thomas Wright's complete 1849 edition followed a now-unlocated working transcript he made while the poem portion was still privately held. A genuine original-1902 Konrath scan, a distinct 1973 Kraus unaltered-reprint scan, and a Wright 1849 scan are cached; the Richmond object is not an original 1902 carrier. A Utrecht finding aid lists microfilm M113 for Add MS 17376 and all seven incipits, but does not establish whole-codex completeness or image quality. Kenyon's public 1900 facsimile volume reproduces only f.92 in the Psalter, outside the target ff.150-220b. The current BL record has no Digitised Content link; no public whole-current-codex facsimile was verified as of 2026-07-17, and an exact Digital Bodleian search found no object for the detached fragment.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for bau0088
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item BAU0088.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR text encoded at Level 1 under the TEI in Libraries guidelines. The encoding contains seven poem divisions, retains their footnote apparatus, and preserves page-break links for omitted preliminary and back matter.

entities.label: Historical U-M page-image set for BAU0088.0001.001
entities.description: The immediate page-image production layer from which U-M says it created the OCR. The XML documents 274 unique, consecutive REF filenames from 00000001.tif through 00000274.tif and describes 274 600 dpi TIFF G4 images. No current image endpoint or retrievable complete bundle was verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Historically described as 274 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images
notes: All 274 filenames occur exactly once in a complete consecutive run.
The exact physical copy of Konrath 1902 scanned by U-M is unidentified.
Unknown survival means current retrievability was not established; it does not mean the images were destroyed.

entities.label: Matthias Konrath, The Poems of William of Shoreham, Part I (1902)
entities.description: EETS Extra Series 86. The complete printed volume has title/preliminary matter, a linguistic and historical introduction, seven edited poems on pp.1-160, notes on pp.161-246, and residual EETS matter. The corpus encoding retains the poem text and in-text apparatus but omits most verbal front and back matter.

bibliographic.citation: Matthias Konrath, ed., The Poems of William of Shoreham, Part I, Early English Text Society, Extra Series 86 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1902).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of William of Shoreham, Part I
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 86
bibliographic.pages: xvii, 246 pp., plus notices and wrappers

entities.label: Kraus Reprint Co. unaltered reprint of Konrath 1902 (1973)
entities.description: A 1973 Kraus Reprint Co. carrier that reproduces Konrath's 1902 EETS Extra Series 86 volume and describes itself as an unaltered reprint produced with EETS permission. The reprint title and notice are new 1973 matter even though the historical edition content is reproduced unchanged.

bibliographic.citation: Matthias Konrath, ed., The Poems of William of Shoreham, Part I, EETS Extra Series 86 (1902; unaltered reprint, Millwood, New York: Kraus Reprint Co., 1973).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of William of Shoreham, Part I
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 86
bibliographic.pages: Reproduces xvii, 246 pp. plus notices and wrappers; adds Kraus reprint matter

notes: catalog_description is the current-schema workaround for an unaltered mechanical reprint carrier; this entity does not claim a new scholarly editing act.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete Richmond scan of the 1973 Kraus reprint
entities.description: A complete 276-page scan contributed by University of California Libraries, with provider OCR and metadata. Kraus's 1973 matter is on PDF pp.5-6; the reprinted poem pp.1-160 map to PDF pp.25-184.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete Google scan of Konrath 1902
entities.description: A complete 285-page Google-sourced scan of an original 1902 edition copy, with provider OCR and metadata. Printed poem pp.1-160 map to PDF pp.28-187.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, The Religious Poems of William de Shoreham (1849)
entities.description: The complete prior seven-poem Percy Society edition, printed from Wright's own working transcript. Wright states that the manuscript was private when he transcribed it and that he could not refer back to the original while the edition was in press.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., The Religious Poems of William de Shoreham, Vicar of Chart-Sutton, in Kent, in the Reign of Edward II, Percy Society 28 (London: Richards, 1849).
bibliographic.title: The Religious Poems of William de Shoreham

entities.label: Thomas Wright's working transcript of the Shoreham poems
entities.description: A modern scholarly intermediary provisionally typed manuscript_witness because the current lineage schema has no transcript entity type. Wright made it while Add MS 17376 remained in private hands and used it for the 1849 edition. Physical form, date of transcription, current location, and survival are unknown.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete Harvard/Google scan of Wright 1849
entities.description: A complete 189-page scan, with provider OCR and metadata. Title page is PDF p.8, preface PDF pp.12-15, and the seven poems PDF pp.16-183.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 17376
entities.description: A fourteenth-century vellum octavo composite. The current British Library record describes a Latin/English Psalter with canticles, the Athanasian Creed, prayers, and related matter in the opening portion, followed from f. 150 by Middle English religious poems and an imperfect doctrinal poem beginning at f. 206. Hanna identifies nine Latin leaves formerly at the front of part I as now separately preserved as Bodleian MS Lat. th. e. 32. Directly inspected historical sources report 220 or 221 leaves.

date_statements.label: Hanna's part-layer proposals
date_statements.value: c.1330-70 for part I; possibly c.1330-48 for poem-bearing part II

notes: The 220/221-leaf disagreement is retained at source level; the current BL record does not supply a leaf count.
The detached Bodleian leaves are recorded as a separate current physical entity.

This reusable entity core contains only intrinsic identity, holding, physical, date, and contents facts; work-specific loci and exclusions belong in scoped relations, access records, evidence, and questions.

physical_description.extent: Current catalog: 1 item; Konrath reports 220 leaves and Kenyon 221

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Lat. th. e. 32
entities.description: Nine Latin leaves that Hanna identifies as formerly at the front of Add MS 17376 part I and now separately preserved under this Bodleian shelfmark.
notes: A dated Digital Bodleian exact-shelfmark query found no public object; that access finding is not part of this intrinsic entity core.
Detached from the historical composite before the currently documented holding arrangement.

entities.label: Ralph Hanna, London Literature, 1300-1380 (2005 publisher excerpt)
entities.description: A ten-page Cambridge University Press excerpt from Hanna’s 2005 monograph. It is a documentary scholarly publication describing the detached front leaves and proposing part-layer dates for Add MS 17376.

bibliographic.citation: Ralph Hanna, London Literature, 1300-1380, Cambridge Studies in Medieval Literature 57 (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 2005), publisher excerpt.
bibliographic.title: London Literature, 1300-1380

notes: This entity represents the downloaded documentary excerpt itself. Its practical evidentiary role does not make it part of the historical transmission path or a local copy of either manuscript witness.

entities.label: Utrecht Index to Microfilms of Medieval English Texts (2014)
entities.description: The complete 177-page Utrecht University Library finding aid compiled from the English Institute inventory and issued in its current PDF form in November 2014. Entry M113 names Add MS 17376 and lists the seven Shoreham-poem incipits.

bibliographic.citation: Bart Jaski, Index to Microfilms of Medieval English Texts (Utrecht University Library, November 2014).
bibliographic.title: Index to Microfilms of Medieval English Texts

notes: This entity represents the downloaded documentary finding aid. It is not microfilm M113 and is not attached to M113 as a cached facsimile or supplied reel scan.

entities.label: Utrecht University Library microfilm M113 of BL Add MS 17376
entities.description: A microfilm entry in Utrecht's medieval English texts finding aid. The entry names Add MS 17376 and lists all seven target incipits. The finding aid does not state whole-codex coverage, polarity, frame count, image quality, or current delivery format.
entities.reproduction_format: Microfilm; polarity, extent, and quality unverified

entities.label: Facsimiles of Biblical Manuscripts in the British Museum (1900)
entities.description: A printed descriptive facsimile publication containing Plate XXII for Add MS 17376, reproducing f. 92 with Psalms 98:1–8 and supplying historical provenance and physical description.

bibliographic.citation: British Museum, Department of Manuscripts, with Frederic G. Kenyon, Facsimiles of Biblical Manuscripts in the British Museum (London, 1900).
bibliographic.title: Facsimiles of Biblical Manuscripts in the British Museum

notes: catalog_description is the current-schema workaround for this descriptive printed facsimile publication; it is not modeled as a new scholarly edition.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete Getty scan of Kenyon 1900
entities.description: A complete 116-page scan of Kenyon's printed facsimile volume. Target-poem image presence is absent: the only Add MS 17376 plate is f.92.

subject.description: Complete XML serialization, including seven poem divisions and omitted-matter PB scaffolding

locators: 274 unique REF values

object.description: Complete Konrath 1902 printed volume represented from wrapper through back wrapper

scope.notes: The XML source description names Konrath 1902 and the 274-image run spans the complete physical publication represented in the XML. The exact scanned copy remains unidentified.

object.description: The seven poem sequence at approximately ff.150-220b; exact part boundaries differ among the BL catalog, DIMEV, and XML milestones
locators: f.150 through f.220b

scope.notes: Transcribes describes the textual basis, not a diplomatic method. Konrath added punctuation, expanded contractions, supplied and omitted words, selectively restored dialectal forms, and made conjectural emendations.

subject.description: 1973 unaltered reprint, including its new reprint matter

scope.notes: The relation records textual/bibliographic versioning, not identity of the 1973 carrier with an original 1902 copy.

object.description: Complete 1973 Kraus reprint carrier, including new reprint matter and reproduced 1902 content

subject.description: Seven-poem text, critical discussion, and right-margin Wright page concordance

object.description: Wright's complete seven-poem edition, printed pp.1-168

scope.notes: This is a supporting prior-edition relationship. Konrath's direct manuscript re-editing remains the primary basis.

object.description: Wright's complete working transcript of those poems

scope.notes: Wright says he relied on this transcript because he could not consult the original while the edition was in press.

subject.description: Working transcript of the complete seven-poem sequence

object.description: Seven poems in the then-private codex, now approximately ff.150-220b

scope.notes: Wright's preface says the poem portion was still private when he made his transcript. This relation does not promote the unfrozen Sibson thesis's more precise later custody narrative.

subject.description: M113 microfilm extent unverified; finding aid reports all seven target incipits

object.description: At least the seven-poem sequence at approximately ff.150-220b is reported present

scope.notes: Do not upgrade this relation to whole-codex completeness until the reel or a supplied scan has been inspected.

subject.description: Plate XXII and its description, complete PDF p.99

scope.notes: Target-poem presence is absent because f.92 precedes the poem sequence beginning f.150. The relation is retained only as supporting whole-carrier evidence.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Repository XML through U-M images and Konrath 1902 to Add MS 17376
primary_transmission_paths.description: The reviewed production path for the corpus artifact. It makes the page-image intermediary explicit and scopes Konrath's manuscript relation to the seven poems rather than the whole codex.

supporting_relationships.label: Downloaded public scan carriers for the immediate edition
supporting_relationships.description: The genuine 1902 scan and the distinct 1973 reprint scan permit comparison with the corpus XML while preserving their different carrier histories.

supporting_relationships.label: Prior Wright edition and its working-transcript route
supporting_relationships.description: Konrath consulted Wright's prior edition; Wright's own production route ran through a now-unlocated transcript made from the private manuscript.

supporting_relationships.label: Reported and partial manuscript reproductions
supporting_relationships.description: Utrecht M113 reportedly includes the target sequence but has unverified extent; Kenyon's downloaded plate is verified but shows only non-target f.92.

rights.component: Exact pinned XML data, transcription, and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME root license uses Unlicense wording while repeatedly calling its subject software; its coverage of inherited XML data, transcription, and markup is not independently established.
notes: No upstream edition, scan, image, or provider contract is cleared through this repository statement.

rights.component: U-M hosted digital text and encoding; collection-level assertion only
rights.basis: The directly inspected CME collection page says the materials are public domain in the United States and displays No Copyright - United States; the exact BAU0088 item page was not directly inspected.
notes: This is not an item-specific open license. The historical XML’s generic availability notice remains a separate layer.

editorial_practices.representation: Editorial punctuation in the printed text
editorial_practices.description: Konrath states that punctuation is editorial.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded forms in the printed text
editorial_practices.description: Konrath states that expansion of manuscript contractions is editorial.

editorial_practices.representation: Square brackets for supplied wording and parentheses for words judged omitted
editorial_practices.description: Konrath’s signs distinguish supplied and omitted wording; consult the edition for exact applications.

editorial_practices.representation: Selective Kentish restoration, emendation, and conjecture
editorial_practices.description: Konrath declines to call the text fully critical but restores selected forms, emends readings, and discusses conjectures in the notes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Proofs, notes, running heads, and doubtful manuscript readings
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall’s proof collation and Bülbring’s reading checks

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 274-image carrier with print text and paratext

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete Konrath carrier including preliminary matter and continuous notes

editorial_practices.source_feature: Konrath folio milestones, including duplicate and non-monotonic values

open_questions.question: Does Utrecht microfilm M113 reproduce the whole codex, and is the reel complete, positive or negative, and available as a full-resolution full-reel scan?
next_steps: Ask Utrecht to inspect M113 and report complete coverage, polarity, frame count, image quality, delivery format, and cost.

open_questions.question: What retention, redistribution, and publication terms would Utrecht attach to a supplied M113 scan?
next_steps: Request explicit written terms separately from any scan quote.

open_questions.question: Do the 274 U-M TIFF files still survive, and can U-M provide a complete package despite the dark IA item?
next_steps: Ask U-M about the historical TIFF set, exact current package, preservation status, request route, and image-specific rights.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy of Konrath 1902 did U-M scan?
next_steps: Seek package logs, item metadata, or U-M production documentation identifying the carrier.

open_questions.question: What copyright history applies to Kraus Reprint Co.’s 1973 title, notice, typography, and other new matter?
next_steps: Establish notice, renewal, authorship, work-for-hire status, and jurisdiction-specific duration before changing the conservative status.

open_questions.question: Was Add MS 17376 digitised in full before the British Library cyber-attack, and does a complete surrogate still survive?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether a prior full surrogate exists and whether it is recoverable; do not infer history from the blank current field.

open_questions.question: What complete-codex service, quote, delivery format, and written publication permission would the British Library offer?
next_steps: Request covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and every leaf if feasible; give ff. 150–220b separately as the BAU0088 association and request explicit terms.

open_questions.question: Does Bodleian MS Lat. th. e. 32 have a public catalog route, unpublished photography, or a requestable complete surrogate under another description?
next_steps: Search current Bodleian catalogs under variants and ask the repository; retain the exact-search zero result only as dated evidence.

open_questions.question: Why do Konrath and Kenyon report 220 and 221 leaves, and can Sibson’s official PDF be frozen before its indexed 222-leaf claim is reconsidered?
next_steps: Obtain and inspect the official Sibson PDF and seek a current repository collation before selecting a count.

open_questions.question: Can the exact source composition behind part VI’s Grosseteste notice be identified?
next_steps: Seek current specialist source analysis; do not assert Grosseteste target authorship or William’s translator role meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Does Wright’s pre-1849 working transcript survive in an archive or private collection?
next_steps: Search Wright papers and institutional catalogs; preserve unknown survival and location until direct evidence appears.

open_questions.question: Was a Konrath Part II ever completed or issued?
next_steps: Check EETS publication records and bibliographies; do not infer an extant continuation from the Part I label.

review_notes: The complete evidence-reviewed graph has exactly fourteen relations: four ordered primary-path relations and ten supporting relations, each classified once.
The historical U-M 274-TIFF layer remains survival/access unknown; the only cached IA response is a 270-byte metadata-only dark-item record with target-work presence absent.
The Richmond carrier is the 1973 Kraus unaltered reprint, represented as catalog_description rather than a false new scholarly edition; its scan is not an original 1902 carrier.
Wright’s 1849 edition reaches Add MS 17376 through his now-unlocated handwritten working transcript; no flattened direct press-to-codex route is asserted.
Utrecht M113 coverage remains unknown even though the finding aid lists all seven incipits.
Kenyon’s publication is target-absent documentary context: its Add MS 17376 plate is f. 92, and its IA facsimile is separately modeled.

Hanna, Utrecht, Kenyon, DIMEV, METS, GND, law, and access-policy downloads are each visible through their own documentary entity/access layers with checksummed local copies; the locally generated deterministic audit is separately visible as a checksummed local-only entity.

The shared Add MS 17376 and Bodleian entity cores contain only intrinsic identity, holding, physical, date, and contents facts. BAU0088-specific loci, exclusions, and target-presence claims remain in relations, access, evidence, and questions.
Sibson-only precise custody, date, and 222-leaf claims remain unpromoted because the official thesis PDF was not frozen and directly inspected.
